review_title: Intuitive and versatile
description: Overall, my experience is very satisfying thanks to its versatility, regular updates, and ease of use.
author: Julien G.
pros: Very intuitive and versatile, allows marketing, email campaigns, sales tracking, and automation easily.
cons: Frequent updates can be overwhelming, making it difficult to adapt and navigate interface changes at times

review_title: Smooth, reliable CMS that plays perfectly with our CRM
description: Overall, it’s been excellent for our team. Reliable, easy to work with, and has some great integrations. It has genuinely streamlined how we operate. It’s taken a lot of friction out of managing content and connecting it back to the rest of the business.
author: khyan A.
pros: What I liked most about HubSpot is how easily it slots into our day-to-day. I can’t comment on pricing, but the usability alone is worth it - it just makes life simpler. I’m an Inside Seller running hundreds of leads through the CRM, and the clean, straightforward layout makes it easy to stay on top of prospects, tasks, and follow-ups. Tasks, in particular, are a non-negotiable for my workflow. I’ve used a few different systems over the years, but HubSpot wins hands-down for its intuitive UI and the way it creates genuinely unified workflows. Being able to link Outlook straight into the platform is a massive help too - keeps everything tidy and in one place.
cons: Occasional guardrails when it comes to seeing some data but that's in the works - updates are implemented and work great out of the gate. not much to complain about to be honest

review_title: Basic CMS that works well with the Hubspot CRm
author: Axel H.
pros: That it is fully integrated with the CRM out of the box.
cons: The A/B testning is weak, allowing you only to try out one page at a time. Hinders you from getting a full view of the impact each page had on the overall goal.

review_title: Seamless integrated marketing platform for your website
description: Overall it's been a pleasure to work with, over the last couple years I have migrated a number of sites from Wordpress environments to Hubspot and it has been a good experience bringing more structure, organization, security and integrated marking to these sites.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Through Hubspot's CMS Hub it's easy to seamlessly integrate and develop your website with all your other marketing activities if you are using Hubspot as your main marketing platform. The CMS system makes it easy with robust themes and a versatile editor, good for beginners and advanced users. You can manage multiple domains/sites and have your analytics with it.
cons: There is often some limitations with the web development, while you do have a high degree of control you are still a little bit constrained within their system and have to adapt to their flow of processes. That said over the past couple years they continue to develop and improve it and I find it has become much more developer friendly as time has gone on.

review_title: Good newsletter solution for small email lists.
description: It has a relatively high learning curve compared to other email services, but you get what you pay for. The service has worked for simple emails.
author: Emmanuel M.
pros: I like that they allow 2,000 free emails per month, so I can keep my supporters up to date on what I am doing in my ministry. It automatically handles unsubscribes, has contact segmenting, auto-fill tokens so I can personalize my emails, and a great text editor to make my email sending easy.
cons: I wish the token service was more robust and easier to use conditional statements with. It doesn't have if/then for the conditionals, at least not with the free version.

review_title: Great CMS for non-developers with some limitations
description: Overall, CMS Hub has helped us build a much more consistent and reliable website. It keeps us on track and makes it a snap to create pages for short-term promotions or urgently-needed webpages. It's a very handy tool and I certainly recommend it.
author: Kristi A.
pros: The greatest thing about the HubSpot CMS is that it's very straightforward and allows just about anyone to create a modern, clean-looking website with modern functionality. The drag-and-drop interface that it currently uses is a massive improvement over the previous functionality, which I would probably not have recommended. The Asset Marketplace allows you to find a lot of pieces and features to put together your own custom (ish) build. It's also (needless to say) an incredible bonus that the website integrates perfectly with HubSpot's CRM.
cons: My least favorite part about using HubSpot CMS is that some things are just not customizable when they ought to be, and some things are not possible to do when they also ought to be. It would also be nice to have the ability to organize pages and posts by folders as well to cut down on clutter when it comes to organizing and keeping track of your website pages.

review_title: Uncovering leads through blog posts and premium content
description: Overall the CMS is very easy to use - whether updating a web page or publishing a new blog post - and provides valuable insights. Having a fully integrated CMS plus CRM is helpful to our sales team as we look to uncover new leads and understand which topics are most interesting to our clients and prospects.
author: Matthew M.
pros: We really appreciate the integration between our website page views, email engagement, and our sales pipeline. Understanding the trends regarding what content on our website is generating the most sessions, page views, and ultimately leads is extremely helpful.
cons: It can be difficult to clean up the contact list from website users who subscribe to our blog, download eBooks, or register for events. Figuring out which contacts are outdated and could be deleted could be improved.

review_title: Easy and Organized
description: Overall, things with HubSpot work well. The minor issues we encounter don't slow down productivity, and they don't get in the way too much. The ease of access makes it an easy choice for our business.
author: Katharine D.
pros: The design is clean and easy to understand. I'm able to clearly access what our clients have done with their accounts, whether it's making a purchase or viewing part of the website.
cons: I get signed out a lot. Not sure if that's a bug in the system or a safety feature, but either way, I feel like I'm constantly having to relog into our company's account. Also, sometimes a tab is opened, and it becomes the new standard setting. Not sure how to get rid of it.

review_title: HubSpot CMS Hub Review
description: Easy to use interface. Customer service is never perfect, but there for a reason and does its' job.
author: Jessieah M.
pros: The seamless integration with HubSpot’s CRM and marketing tools. Unlike other platforms, everything is built to work together—website, email, automation, and customer data—so you can personalize experiences and scale faster without duct-taping different systems.
cons: The higher price compared to basic platforms. It’s not the cheapest option, but if you’re serious about growth and want powerful tools without the mess of plugins, the value is absolutely worth it.

review_title: One of the best CRMs
author: Emily D.
pros: Very easy to use and there is little to no learning curve. Love the CRM aspects of Hubspot and the reporting has been extremely helpful. The Breeze AI assistant has been super fun to utilize and has sped up my workload when utilizing HubSpot.
cons: I wish there were better ways for the program to recognize duplicate entries of potential clients. I do wish there was a way to better organize the home page (maybe this is user error on my part) but it can be a lot to look at when I am trying to find something in the system.

review_title: Easy to use CRM tool
author: Despoina M.
pros: The ease of use and how fast it is to pick up.
cons: The fact that the first response of their support is sharing a knowledge article instead of responding exactly on what you are looking for. This is not efficient given that I am reaching out on their support as I was not able to find this info myself or did not understand their article.

review_title: Practical answers to website personalization needs
description: Understanding our customers behavior on our site enables us to react accordingly. We stand out to be counted in the online marketplace courtesy of CMS Hub.
author: Gabriel D.
pros: The prowess in creating customized experiences for website visitors is well displayed here. This way, creating a lasting trust and loyalty for our customers and increasing customer lifetime value. It is easy to customize which helps us to analyze user data and examine their changing needs and behavior while they interact with our site.
cons: Things are flowing in the right direction. I highly feel that HubSpot CMS Hub is headed to the right direction.

review_title: A user-friendly system with lots of possibilities
author: Sarah T.
pros: The ease of use is the main feature that appeals to me - I've used various other CRMs and CMSs and they haven't been nearly as intuitive as Hubspot is! In particular the new drag and drop editor makes life so much simpler and means I can customise as many email and webpage templates as required without fiddling around behind the scenes (although the Design Manager is also fairly simple to use when you need to). The fact that the CRM and CMS integrate with each other is also a great feature as it provides excellent activity tracking data and allows us to identify when prospects are engaging with our content in order to make contact at the right time. Reporting data is easily accessible and can be sliced and diced to your requirements by means of custom reports, charts etc.
cons: Initially we struggled to get Hubspot fully integrated with our Outlook email system but now that we have implemented Office 365 (a separate project) the features seem to work much better.

review_title: Great for Now AND the Future!!
author: Crystal N.
pros: Having all my marketing efforts in one place talking the same lingo has greatly increased my efficiency
cons: There is a bit of a learning curve but once you understand it’s very easy plus there are so many helpful resources it’s simple to gain guidance

review_title: Great tool when website is fully integrated
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use. Hubspot really made their CMS as easy to use as possible. Also being able to see the changes in real-time is a huge time saver rather than needing to input changes and then preview them in a new window.
cons: being able to change text in the preview window rather than edit bay would be a huge time saver.

review_title: All hubspot products are nice
description: Compared to most use website like Wordpress, it's definitely more efficient to use Hubspot CMS Hub. You can achieve more but doing less and let it do the job for you.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It helps if you have a website and want to see how it is performing. Reporting on traffic, sales, and else are super easy to get. It is also a fantastic tool to capture leads. If you know how to use it, it will help you higher the performance of your website. It is also flexible with content and templates and all.
cons: Pricing is not accessible to all companies. It's relatively expensive for what they offer. It is a another job to watch if it is really made for you or if you should choose another software maybe with a bit less flexibility but cheaper.

review_title: It’s Expensive But Good
description: Pretty good. Customer support and using one platform makes it worthwhile for me. However if you want a more robust solution - eg a really good chatbot - you may need to go for specialist platforms.
author: Simone P.
pros: I’ve used a few CMS programs before and I don’t see a huge amount of difference between Hubspot and them, other than the price! However it has many features that do 70% of what you’d like it to do. The alternative is to purchase 4-5 specialist programs that are built to do 90% of what you’d like it to do, but then you’re jumping between multiple programs. Hubspot is good if you want the simplicity of 1 central place. I will say the customer support is fantastic. That’s worth a big chunk of the investment.
cons: I do wish they’d offer more services in house. They have an affiliate network that you’re often pushed to, whereas for the price I think they should offer more of these services themselves. For example I’m relaunching a whole new website and they don’t have a team on board who can help me do this. They offer tools and advice but any action is referred to an affiliate.

review_title: Great Management System
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Very user friendly and flexible for diy setup and management.
cons: Some additional features are limited with out add on features

review_title: HubSpot CMS review
description: As difficult as they sometimes can be, the massive amount of integrations available can make the CRM a really powerful tool for anyone working with marketing or sales. Through our clients I often see realisations of how much is possible with HubSpot CRM and the entire ecosystem.
author: Daniel J.
pros: With HubSpot CMS being super user friendly it's always a breeze to onboard our clients and with everything gathered at one spot, it allows them to instantly see the value of a single ecosystem.
cons: Setting up custom integrations is not as easy as it could be, and keeping an overview of current integrations can be a little troublesome.

review_title: Hubspot Pages
author: Derek G.
pros: The UI is very clean and easy to understand. I often clone an existing page to save design time and like that I can easily add custom styling to a particular page in its advanced settings to avoid wrangling what has become a rather large set of stylesheets.
cons: My only complaint of note is the size of the html editor panel. While it is great to see changes update live it is also more difficult than it needs to be to navigate the html in a narrow panel. Maybe a detachable panel that can be sized by the user would be a better option?

review_title: Great Product
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Verx intuitive, easy to use. Lot of functionalities that make it extremely necessary.
cons: Very expensive for a SME with small buget
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: HubSpot is great and easy to use
author: Luke B.
pros: I appreciate HubSpot the most because it is my one-stop-shop for running our organization. We are a member run Institute so have an organized place to keep all member information and communication records is beyond necessary.
cons: Nothing missing. Just keep the integrations coming with new apps! We use Eventleaf has a ticketing service so would love to see that integrated.

review_title: One of the best marketing tool!
author: Benjamin L.
pros: Hubspot helps us automate our campaigns easily and provides all the statistics we need to evaluate our results. The various tools for creating emails, landing pages, CTAs and forms are really easy to use. I like having my CRM and marketing tools linked to the same place, it allows us to customize our campaigns at a higher level. The Hubspot academy is great and really helpful.
cons: A negative side of Hubspot is the high additional cost to add different extra features.

review_title: Intuitive platform for digital designers
author: Katie S.
pros: Modular structure makes updating multiple pages and emails easy, and it's straightforward to make content editable for non-coders.
cons: At the moment it's not possible to use contact properties in conditional statements. I wish support knew more about their custom coding language.

review_title: Easy to use and reliable CRM
description: I like Hubspot because the CRM integrates with other apps. Easy to use as well as keep records of calls and tagging feature
author: Jean N.
pros: Helpful in keeping client information in order as well as sharing and updating records
cons: No real cons. We use it at a very base level so all features are not yet utilised
